## Release — backlog-campaign v0.15.0

Two initiatives land together in this release — **Companion Substrate Closure** (ADR-011 + ADR-012)
and the **Mercure Parity Program** (ADR-013) — plus the BREAKING autonomy default flip and a new
coverage-regression gate. blackhole is now *autonomous mercure over a shared artifact substrate*
with measurable enforcement/artifact parity.

### ✨ Companion Substrate Closure (ADR-011 + ADR-012)

- **Implement-time accretion control** — the Reuse-Check gate's aperture widened to a repo-wide
  existence search plus a rule-of-three duplication threshold; Scout Check unified by diff scope.
- **Repo-convention schema precedence** — new `detect-doc-schema.sh` detects whether a repo uses
  mercure's or blackhole's ADR/INDEX schema and emits matching artifacts.
- **Durable decision memory** — implementers emit `decision_records[]`; the orchestrator banks them
  (single-writer) into `documentation/reference/decision-log.md`.
- **Human-approved design promotion** — a design a human approves now promotes verbatim (fixes a
  live bug where design-track blocks never resumed); the planner never self-grades.
- **Active Constraints write path** — `ARCHITECTURE.md`'s Active Constraints finally gets a writer,
  gated by a 3-question cross-cutting heuristic.

### ✨ Mercure Parity Program (ADR-013)

- **Living parity matrix** — `documentation/audits/mercure-parity-matrix.md`, ~82 rows mapping every
  mercure enforcement mechanism to its blackhole equivalent, self-validated by a new `V-PMATRIX-01`
  verify check; `prj-mercure-sync` is its sole writer.
- **Adoption Lens v2 + dual-mode sync** — tiered ADOPT / ADAPT-async / N/A classification; release-
  and backlog-mode sweeps.
- **Threat-Model & Performance-Budget machinery** — four new V-codes (`V-THREAT-02/03`,
  `V-PERF-01/02`) with conditional planner sections and reviewer audits.
- **Merge/delivery hardening** — spec-drift-at-merge check, delivery-boundary evidence, and a
  BLOCKING acceptance-criteria gate.
- **`parity` self-audit hunt kind** — and the first matrix-driven backlog sweep (filed #306).

### ✨ Coverage-regression gate (#306)

New `V-TEST-09` (BLOCK): implementer sessions now verify that per-file coverage on changed files
does not regress against the pre-change baseline, reusing the existing runner-detection heuristic.

### ⚠️ BREAKING — Autonomy default flip (ADR-012 E5)

`.blackhole/config.json`'s `autonomy.enabled` now defaults to `true` in the shipped config template.
Two thinking-route classes activate, and one stays deliberately pinned off:

- **Design autonomy** (`design_autonomy: true`, now live) — design-track issues may promote directly
  to an ADR inside their own PR when blind-critic scoring and the deterministic
  `scripts/design-aggregate.ts` verdict agree. The planner never self-certifies this path.
- **Analyze routing** (`analyze_routing: true`, now live) — large (`size:l`+) or design-flagged
  issues automatically get an evidence-gathering `analyze` pass before planning.
- **Brainstorm routing** (`brainstorm_routing: false`, pinned OFF) — brainstorm-routed issues close
  as *satisfied-by-children* with no reviewable PR (terminal-closure semantics, ADR-010 D3). That
  product judgement stays opt-in and is **not** activated by this flip.

The `never_bypass` floor (`["destructive", "credentials", "epic-go-no-go"]`) is **unchanged** —
those actions still force human escalation regardless of confidence score.

**Observable change:** campaigns will stop pausing at design gates they previously always paused at,
and will begin dispatching `needs_analysis` routes for `size:l`+ and design-flagged issues.

### ⚠️ Verification status — read before running a campaign with autonomy live

The flip shipped **without a prior green campaign** (the milestone's Entry-Gate green-campaign row
was waived by explicit maintainer decision). Everything passed the full structural gate
(`bun run verify` 30/30, `bun test` green, build clean) and unit-level review, but the autonomous
dispatch paths have **not yet been exercised end-to-end**. The T3 verification — one live campaign
confirming its four criteria (substantive escalations only; zero unintended brainstorm dispatch;
design gates resolve/block correctly; analyze dispatch fires for a qualifying issue) — **remains
outstanding**. Treat the first live run on this version as that verification pass.

### ⏪ Rollback

The autonomy behavior is a single switch: set `autonomy.enabled: false` in `.blackhole/config.json`
to restore the pre-flip dispatch behavior exactly. (The rest of the release — matrix, V-codes,
schema detection, decision log — is additive and independently safe.)
